title: internal-event FIFO drain
manager: Internal events (raised by the machine itself during a macrostep) are queued and drained first-in-first-out before the macrostep completes, so a cascade of self-triggered transitions runs in a defined order to a stable point. FIFO drain is what makes run-to-completion actually complete predictably; the microstep bound is what stops an ill-formed cascade from draining forever and hanging the host.
cs: internal-event FIFO queue drained within the macrostep (W6.71); bounded by the microstep limit (W6.45); the ordering is part of the C2 conformance surface.
